threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'overground' is a fancy way to describe the weather. It’s just another line, mate, not a rooftop bar.
Caught the 59 last night; a bloke sat down and offered his half-eaten kebab like it was a fancy snack. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was really home when I saw the guy selling handmade coffee outside the U-Bahn, he remembers your order and always adds a biscuit.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst while a street musician plays an accordion next to a pop-up gallery. It’s a vibe you won’t find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they ask for hot sauce at a taco truck and don’t flinch at the spice—it's all about the burn.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to get his dog off the tracks at Civic Center today. Muni was delayed for 30 minutes—only in this city, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we’re all about Gaudí; they miss our hidden plazas where locals gather, sipping vermut and sharing stories, that's the real Gràcia.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell newcomers by their confusion at the lack of direct trains to Gràcia. They always ask for the quickest route—it’s never straightforward.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the 'Overground' instead of the 'Gospel Oak line'. Real locals know it’s a bit more specific than just the train.
Saw a guy in a full suit talking loudly about his first date on the 59 last night. He was convinced he was the star of a romcom, everyone just tried not to laugh.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I spotted a guy feeding a flock of pigeons while juggling coffee cups at Hermannplatz. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a busker with a grand piano on the street, playing Chopin while kids dance around in the rain. Pure Berlin magic.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can name all the taco trucks along 16th St and have a favorite spot that isn’t La Taqueria.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 31 stop mid-sentence, threw his arms up, and yelled, 'Even the Muni knows when it’s time to quit!' Classic Tenderloin moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think Gràcia is all about the parks and plazas, but the real charm is in the narrow streets with the hidden murals and quirky shops.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still order their coffee in large sizes and ask for soy milk at every café, oblivious to the local tradition of a simple cortado.
